channel.title: Morgan Mayday
channel.copyright: Anticopyright. We all live in the public domain.

item.title: From Here to Tomorrow
item.description:
I can’t see the line.

because there is, right? there is some line, some sequence of events and actions, that I can take, that I can advocate, to make a Better World. I believe that; I have to believe that.

but I can’t see it, can’t hold it all in my mind at once, picture what needs to be done. and that scares me. I lie awake, some nights, staring at the wall, asking–what should I do? What can I do?

item.title: sharp edges
item.description:
Estimates put the number of civilian-owned firearms in the US at 500 million. The US population is, at time of writing, estimated at around 342 million bodies.

i press on the disassembly plate, pull the slide off, set it down, and hand you the receiver. see, she can’t shoot, taken apart like this. feel the contour of her grip, the featherlight weight without the slide. there’s no need to worry, honey, you can’t hurt me with her like this. hold her while we talk, yeah? holding a stripped-down receiver, there’s this … vulnerability, almost, to the gun. inert, unable to fire, like a knife without a blade, a hand without movement, a voice without words. this thing that is made for the express purpose of violence and death, and she’s naked and open under your hands, rendered near–but never wholly–harmless, while you care for her. can you feel it?

item.title: but, instead, something else.
item.description:
Required reading: Not A Person.

Search. if not a person, then what?

you could go the route of the dolls. could disavow personhood, crunch down on your agency, be an object acted upon, rather than an actor in your own right. you could be a tool, an implement, something in service of another. it’d be safer, easier, comfier, being provided for like that and providing in turn, as required of you. you wouldn’t even have to be a person. someone else can do the heavy bit. you can interface with Consensus with an intermediary, rather than direct.

item.title: Morgan Mayday's Quick Vegan Cookbook
item.description:
Note 29/03/2026: God I need to rewrite this. I have more foods I eat now it’s great

The first version of this guide is being published 05 February 2026, and will be continually updated as I acquire more good recipes. If you have a good, easy recipe, email it to me at me@morganmay.day and I might give it a shot :)

Why?

I’ve detailed my reasons why, but you may have your own! I won’t waste time speculating.

item.title: killjoy
item.description:
people in real life: hey man hows it going

various (meme)

There is a tendency to assign neutrality to the masculine form of words in English, mostly in slang–“hey, man,” “hey, dude,” “king shit”–though it shows up sometimes in general phrasing still, feminist pushes mostly have made things like “fireman” over “firefighter” obsolete. This is, overall, something most people do, and something most people find pretty harmless. A lot of people will go through their lives not thinking about it, or thinking about it a tiny bit at most.

item.title: To Read, Perchance to Dream
item.description:
Who are you, when the lights are out and nobody’s watching?

If you’ve been in broader plural circles before, adjacent at all to what we call “pop plur”, you’ve met fictives, generally of characters from media. This is something some people–or rather, some People–judge plural folk as lesser as a whole for. This is also adjacent to the endo/exo divide as a concept: systems saying “well, I have a reason to be like this, but they don’t so they’re not real/worthy of respect, they’re doing it for internet points.” Respectability politics is an unwinnable game.

item.title: Do As You Will
item.description:
Lock in. Lock in. If you lock in, you can do anything.

Northernlion

We say it a lot. Do as you will. Some people interpret it as disapproval, which is a fascinating phenomenon to me. It’s not disapproval, not inherently.

What do we mean, do as you will? It’s simple. Do, as in take action, as you, as in you, who You are, will, as in the future and the force of will. When I say “do as you will,” you could interpret that as “you can do whatever you want forever,” or “you’re going to do what you’re going to do,” or “do whatever you decide to do,” or “do what you want to.”

item.title: 5, 10, and a Little Bit of Magic
item.description:
Note 29/03/2026: Pending more coherent rewrite.

I’ve been talking, right, about writing a spotter’s guide to 5&10s for a while, and it hasn’t really been flowing, and that’s fine. It’s frustrating, admittedly, but that’s a me problem.

I was talking, last night, with my colleague, and the topic of writing in general came up, and I said something and then I realized this morning that it’d been where I’ve gone wrong.

item.title: Taking Relationships Seriously, and the Importance of Living Models
item.description:
Note 29/03/2026: I am not happy with the writing, but the principles are still about right.

Shoutout, as usual, to my colleague morrigan. Most of these thoughts were cohered with or to it last night.

I sigh. “Listen,” I say, gesture as if I’m indicating the puff of heat into the midnight air, “you gotta promise not to get weird or attacked, yeah? Cause what I’m about to say is…”

item.title: Hacking Yourself Does, Technically, Work
item.description:
When I say I’m hacking something together, that’s a very specific statement.

To create something on something else, to patch together a solution from disparate pieces onto an existing workflow, that’s something I do all the time in computer science, and every time I do it, I later regret it. I’m not gonna stop, because it’s often the most efficient way for me to learn–for example, I learned to write Nix by editing .nix files with no fucking clue what I was doing and then parsing the error messages–but it is bad practices.

item.title: Strategy for the Dying Future
item.description:
Note 29/03/2026: This was written before ICE’s invasion of Minneapolis, and to try to galvanise hopeful action as opposed to doomerism. I do not think this is good writing anymore, from a strategic standpoint, but I’m leaving it up until I replace it.

I stop laughing, say, “no, really,” finish pouring my drink and sit down across from you at the kitchen table.

The concept of a probability well is simple. When X type of thing happens, it makes it easier for X type of thing to happen, which makes it easier for X type of thing to happen, which et cetera et cetera et cetera you get the gist.

item.title: Empathy, People, and You, or: the Flesh Racket
item.description:
With thanks, deep and irrevocable, to morrigan.

Required reading: Not A Person.

“The nature of empathy and sympathy,” I don’t say, “that I’ve met very few people who understand, is that they’re rarely-to-never going to be exactly right. I don’t really think of myself as having sympathy, because sympathy is understanding how others feel—I can guess, and I can guess very, very well; my models are generally really good, but I will never be physically inside someone else’s head. The mistake of sympathy and empathy is thinking you’re always right.”

item.title: Emotions vs Reason, and Self Control
item.description:
Association with To Desire, amongst other things.

Minds, broadly, react to stimuli. The human animal reacts to stimuli in one way: thought.

For the purposes of this piece, subconscious thought is any electrical impulse in the brain that results in consequence without being registered in what we call the register. These most often will be things like ducking at a loud noise, yanking your hand away from a hot stove, or flinching from a blow–sometimes, you will have them registered, but most of the time, the neuromuscular impulse happens without consulting you. These are things we would consider on the layer of cognitive hardware–things that do not bother to be registered, because doing so wastes time that, evolutionarily, not wasting leads to a higher survival rate.

item.title: Not A Person
item.description:
With thanks to Ra, for talking about this in a way that changed my life, and morrigan, for introducing me to its work, amongst other things.

Recommended reading here.

Personhood is a tool of violence used by Authority, a perpetual threat used to separate Desirables and Undesirables. When any Person considers a group or being Undesirable, they often decry that group or being as not a person, as subhuman, as no better than animals. You can see this happening–well, anywhere you look, really, but most definitely in USAmerican politics as we speak.

item.title: To Desire
item.description:
Note 29/03/2026: Updated a bit of phrasing.

“I don’t want to do this” is a very simple statement.

We live in a world where we have to do things we find unpleasant.
I do not like brushing my teeth. I also have to brush my teeth.
It’s worse, for a lot of folk in school or assignment-based workspaces. A lot of the time, you have to suffer through boring, routine, difficult but not challenging, or overly challenging tasks, and those suck. I don’t like doing them. They do not bring me joy; this is why I’m not currently planning on going back to college.

item.title: Don't Apologise To Me
item.description:
Note 29/03/2026: Pending rewrite.

Note 19/11/2025: Correction for the unapology, added by Caroline.

‘Sorry’ is all well and good, but what are you going to do about it?
Let me explain. There are three kinds of apologies.

One: the superficial apology. “Oop, sorry” is the most benign form of this, running into someone and apologising and moving on is, like, just a generally nice thing to do. However, a lot of people extrapolate this into a counterproductive form.
Generally, apologising for something that doesn’t matter just obligates the other conversational participants to comfort you. The more self-flagellating the apology, the worse this is, because it carries over into the way I approach other apologies from you in future. If you do something that’s, for lack of a better phrase, kinda sucks, I’ll point it out to you if I trust you not to self-flagellate about it and just to use the data. If you’ve done something that hurts or irritates me, the last thing I’m going to want is to have to comfort you out of a spiral. I’ll do it, because as a general rule I think that not doing it would be incompatible with who I want to be, but I will not be bringing data to you in future.

item.title: Agency, Manipulation, Coercion, and Abuse
item.description:
Note 29/03/2026: Rewritten with epistemic corrections.

People conflate manipulation, coercion, and abuse. This is a major mistake.

Manipulation requires the concealment of information. This can be an outright lie or an omission. Manipulation isn’t … I would hesitate to call it outright bad, I don’t think weapons are bad, but it is, fundamentally, a weapon. To manipulate someone is to try to funnel their agency through yours.

This isn’t to say you need to be perfectly transparent with everyone always. You’re allowed to have secrets, and you live in 2026 and are reading my website, you have enemies. Controlling your outbound information is necessary for security–I don’t want Joe Fascist to know anything about me. My friends don’t need to know everything in my life and mind–sometimes, information just isn’t relevant, or is private. The line is blurry, of course, and what is private depends on your own security standards.

item.title: 404 Page Not Found
item.description:
That page couldn’t be found.

Back to homepage.

item.title: Glossary
item.description:
Quick sidebar: all of these are the best working definitions I have to communicate with. Anything marked “borrowed from the void” is sourced from here. I don’t use all terms the same way, so I’m just marking the ones I’m using verbatim or slightly edited for brevity/interreferentiality.

This is, for lack of a better term, vibespace. Caveat emptor.

The Abyss

The metaphysical representation of the entropic probability well, the sum of its generals and more.
